steps: Monitor blood glucose levels regularly as directed by your healthcare team.
Inspect feet daily for any cuts, blisters, or swelling.
Maintain regular visits with your endocrinologist and primary care provider.
Ensure annual eye exams and kidney function tests are performed.
Follow a balanced meal plan that manages carbohydrate intake to help control blood sugar.
Incorporate regular physical activity into your daily routine.
Recognize signs of hyperglycemia (high blood sugar) or hypoglycemia (low blood sugar) and have an action plan.
Seek medical attention for non-healing wounds or sudden vision changes.

reviewNotes: Aligned with v1.1 schema. Neutral tone ensured. No specific insulin dosages mentioned.
